title: At the Train Station
description: Learn how to buy train tickets and deal with delays at a Dutch train station.

content.text: Welcome! Dutch trains ('de trein') are famous for connecting the whole country. In this lesson, you will learn how to buy tickets, find your platform, and handle delays.

data.phrase: Ik wil graag een kaartje naar...
data.meaning: I would like a ticket to...

content.title: Asking About Time with 'Hoe laat...?'
content.explanation:
To ask about time, use 'Hoe laat...?' (What time...?):

Hoe laat vertrekt de trein? = What time does the train leave?

'Vertrekt' comes from 'vertrekken' (to depart). You do not need to memorize the conjugation yet, just remember the full phrase!

data.meaning: What time does the train leave?

content.title: Quick Practice: Buy a Ticket
content.expected_concept: The user should ask for a ticket using 'Ik wil graag een kaartje naar...' followed by a city name, and specify single or return with 'Een enkeltje, alstublieft' or 'Een retourtje, alstublieft'

content.title: Buying a Ticket
content.setting: You are at Amsterdam Central Station. You need to buy a ticket to Utrecht.

conversation_flow.title: Greet and ask for a ticket to Utrecht
Choose single or return
conversation_flow.chatbot_message: Een kaartje naar Utrecht. Enkeltje? Retourtje?

conversation_flow.title: Ask which track using 'Welk spoor?'
conversation_flow.chatbot_message: Een enkeltje naar Utrecht. Dat is tien euro. Alstublieft!

data.meaning: Does the train have a delay?

content.title: Asking 'Do I have to...?' with 'Moet ik...?'
content.explanation:
Use 'Moet ik...?' to ask if you need to do something:

Moet ik overstappen? = Do I need to transfer?

The answer will be 'Ja' (yes) or 'Nee' (no).

You already know 'Kan ik...?' (Can I...?) from Lesson 2. Now you have two useful question patterns!

content.title: Quick Practice: Ask About Delays
content.expected_concept: The user should ask about delays using 'Heeft de trein vertraging?' or ask about transfers using 'Moet ik overstappen?'

content.title: Dealing with a Delay
content.setting: Your train is delayed. Ask a station employee for information.

conversation_flow.title: Greet and ask about the delay
Ask if you need to transfer
Thank them using 'Bedankt voor uw hulp'

content.text: Uitstekend! You have completed all five essential Dutch lessons. You can now shop at a supermarket, order herring, visit a café, ask for directions, and navigate the train station. Goede reis!
